stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 31% and 48%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $55K in good weather patterns, $30K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $162K in good weather patterns, $61K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $213K in good weather patterns, $84K in bad weather patterns. corn: $246K in good weather patterns, $54K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.4: best option is soybeans with EV=$135.6K. The optimal choice switches: soybeans at P(good)=0.31, corn at P(good)=0.48. EV range of best option: [124.0, 146.2].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: soybeans at P(good)=0.31, corn at P(good)=0.48.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
